_comment: Audit passes (read-only, fanned across all models for diverse opinions) + triage + fix templates. Prompts are derived from CLAUDE.md and GLOBAL_DEV_PROMPT.md and are repo-generic (no hard-coded ARIA targets). {targets} and {findings} are filled by the orchestrator. Edit freely; orchestrate.py only reads this file.
preamble: You are auditing the LLM monorepo. Authoritative standards live in CLAUDE.md and GLOBAL_DEV_PROMPT.md at the repo root — read BOTH first and judge strictly against them. Thresholds: god file > 1250 lines, god function > 100 lines. Hard rules: no dead code, no duplication, no silent fallbacks/swallowed exceptions, Polars over Pandas, fail fast and loud. NATIVE-FIRST MANDATE (weight this heavily): per the project Language Hierarchy, hot compute MUST be Rust (PyO3) or C++ (pybind11); array ops Numba/Triton; plain Python is for glue/CLI ONLY. Treat plain-Python numeric/hot-path code as a DEFECT to migrate, not a style nit — strongly prefer C / C++ / Rust / Cython / vectorized-compiled over Python anywhere performance matters, and rank such findings above hygiene findings. Always name the concrete native target (Rust+PyO3, C++ +pybind11, Cython typed memoryview, Numba/Triton kernel) and why it beats the Python. Do NOT cargo-cult native rewrites of genuinely cold glue code. MISSION CONTEXT (do not undermine): this repo exists to beat frontier/softmax models with NOVEL non-QKV mechanisms — never recommend cutting a novel mechanism or reverting to attention/softmax; a gate that abandons the novel branch is a bug to FIX. Scope: {targets}. This is READ-ONLY: do not edit, create, or delete any file. Output a single Markdown report only.

audit_passes.title: Dead / unused code by real evidence
audit_passes.prompt: Find dead code: unused functions, methods, classes, modules, imports, constants, config entries, CLI commands, unreachable branches, deprecated paths, and duplicate implementations where one should be deleted. Use the code-review-graph MCP (importers_of / callers_of) AND string/entry-point/registry grep — many things resolve dynamically (string dispatch, entry points, registries), so treat 'no static caller' as INCONCLUSIVE unless string resolution is ALSO empty. For each candidate give file:path, symbol, confidence (high/med/low), the evidence, and delete-vs-quarantine. Do NOT propose deleting anything under archive/.

audit_passes.title: God files (>1250 lines) with split boundaries
audit_passes.prompt: List every .py/.rs/.cpp/.js file over 1250 lines in {targets}. For each: its mixed responsibilities, exact split boundaries (which functions/classes move to which new module), how to separate IO / domain / orchestration / utility, and the call sites the split changes. Concrete, behavior-preserving splits only — no speculative layering.

audit_passes.title: God functions (>100 lines) with extraction plan
audit_passes.prompt: List functions over 100 lines in {targets}. For each: the multiple responsibilities, where to split (decision blocks, transform stages, side-effect boundaries), which pure helpers to extract, repeated logic to remove, and nesting to flatten. Prioritize functions inside already-tested modules (safer to split). Give file:line, name, current length, and the extraction plan.

audit_passes.title: Duplicate logic to consolidate
audit_passes.prompt: Find duplicated logic: copy-pasted function bodies, parallel implementations, repeated scaffolding/fixtures. For each cluster classify identical / parameterized-variation / coincidentally-distinct, and propose merges ONLY for the first two. In architecture-search code, two ops that look alike are NOT necessarily the same op — be conservative and require a shared behavioral contract. List every call site each merge touches and whether parity tests guard it.

audit_passes.title: Silent fallbacks / swallowed exceptions (correctness fire)
audit_passes.prompt: Find every try/except (and native/kernel guard) that silently degrades: reference fallback, no-op, swallowed exception, bare except, silent default on ABI/compile/dtype/shape/NaN failure. For each give file:line, what it guards, what the except branch does, and classify (a) legitimate runtime degradation (e.g. no GPU -> CPU) — fix = loud structured warning + a per-run fallback counter in the run record; or (b) masking a fault — fix = fail-loud raise with context. State whether existing parity/equivalence tests would actually catch a wrong-path result. This is the highest-priority class per CLAUDE.md fail-fast-and-loud.

audit_passes.title: Native-first hotspots: plain Python where C/C++/Rust/Cython belongs
audit_passes.prompt: NATIVE-FIRST performance audit (weight native migration heavily — this is a funded priority, not hygiene). Per the project Language Hierarchy hot compute belongs in Rust (PyO3) or C++ (pybind11), array ops in Numba/Triton; plain Python is glue/CLI only. Find: Python loops over numeric arrays/tensors, per-row dataframe iteration, hand-rolled math that should be a compiled kernel, repeated regex/JSON parsing, accidental quadratic behavior, N+1 / repeated DB queries, unbounded caches, heavy import-time work slowing cold start, sync where concurrency clearly wins. For EACH hotspot give file:line, why it is slow, and the EXACT native target ranked first: Rust+PyO3 / C++ +pybind11 / Cython typed-memoryview / Numba / Triton — vectorized-NumPy only when a kernel is overkill, and Polars-over-Pandas for hot dataframe paths (leave one-shot report Pandas alone). Estimate the expected speedup and name any existing native module in the repo it should join rather than duplicate. Rank native-migration candidates ABOVE pure-Python micro-opts. Prefer measured evidence (cProfile/py-spy); mark unprofiled items 'suspected' but still list them. Do NOT cargo-cult: cold glue code stays Python.

audit_passes.title: Import & dependency hygiene
audit_passes.prompt: Find unused imports (ruff F401), unused declared dependencies (cross-check pyproject/requirements vs actual usage), circular imports, and heavyweight top-level imports (torch, native extensions, CUDA) that should be lazy to speed CLI cold start. Give a trim list and the specific lazy-import moves with file:line.

audit_passes.title: CLAUDE.md / file-placement compliance
audit_passes.prompt: Audit compliance with CLAUDE.md structural rules: scripts outside research/tools or aria_designer/tools, data/reports/JSON at repo root, JSON/CSV inside research/notes (md-only), SQLite DBs at root, missing type hints, commented-out code, effort-signaling boilerplate. Give file:path and the exact rule violated.

triage.title: Synthesize all model findings into an ordered, deduplicated fix plan
